Is anyone else freaking out because everything with this one is still so uncertain?!? I feel like all the others in the past couple years, there was so much more pinpointing, this one is so uncertain!!

The biggest problem the weather people are struggling with is that there has never been a hurricane tracked with technology that originated where this one did. Please, correct me if I'm wrong......there was a direct hit to Tampa in 1921, but we still don't know if it came in this way, starting IN the Gulf of Mexico. Everything Tampa has lucked out on in the past has originated in the Atlantic and not cleared the land below us, like Cuba, Puerto Rico, Caribbean Islands.

Here's a suggestion to ensure your furry friend's safety during severe tropical storms. Consider purchasing a small pool or inflatable item for under $10 from Walmart or a similar store. Place it in your garage or a secure area of your home. If your garage is noisy and you have a small dog, opt for a toddler pool. You can still use sod or pee pads, but remember to work with your pet and reassure them. Playing soft music during the storm and consulting with your veterinarian can also be helpful. There are various options available, such as special vests, blankets, and medications.
By placing some sod, grass, and perhaps a bit of their waste, you can create a designated area for them to relieve themselves. This will help them sense that it's their space. Place a snack nearby and walk with them to the pool, supporting them as needed. Praise your dog when they use it as their bathroom. This simple measure can provide peace of mind and ensure everyone's safety during inclement weather. Feel free to share this tip with other pet owners, and if you have a different suggestion, please share it as well. We're all in this together. Good luck! The pi k is a soft small sandbox
